title: plectranthus urticifolius
intro: Plectranthus urticifolius, also known as the Cape Tealeaf or Bushman's Tea, is a medicinal plant native to southern Africa. It has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory function, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Plectranthus urticifolius can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
